findingsNew.title: README labels nested runtime infrastructure as a related skill
Hub frontmatter grants broad tools despite routing-only behavior
findingsNew.scopeProof: Limited to hub frontmatter; registry mutating surfaces map to artifact-producing mode packets or improvement hosts.
findingsNew.recommendation: Narrow hub-level allowed-tools to routing-only needs or document why parent hub intentionally exposes the union of child-mode tools before packet selection.

findingsNew.title: Secondary hub docs still report pre-reunification version metadata
findingsNew.scopeProof: Checked current hub version sources, README, root playbook, and representative playbook scenarios; route fields remained current while version metadata did not.
findingsNew.recommendation: Update secondary doc version metadata and coverage note to 2.0.0.0 or mark them historical.

findingsNew.title: Adding another workflow lacks a single maintainer checklist
findingsNew.scopeProof: Checked hub SKILL.md, README, mode-registry.json, hub-router.json, description.json, and graph-metadata.json. Current routing is coherent; the risk is future synchronized edits.
findingsNew.recommendation: Add a short 'Adding a workflow mode' checklist to the hub docs naming every required surface and the drift-guard test.

findingsNew.title: Research-charter manual tests still cite stale Step 5a
findingsNew.counterevidenceSought: Checked whether current loop_protocol still exposes Step 5a for Validate Research Charter; current canonical docs define Step 7a and SKILL.md cites Step 7a.
findingsNew.alternativeExplanation: The manual-testing docs likely preserve an older step number, but they are live operator instructions and now misdirect testers.
findingsNew.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade if the playbook is explicitly historical/non-operational or tooling aliases Step 5a to Step 7a.

findingsNew.title: Registry filename contract conflicts between top-level docs and reducer writes
findingsNew.counterevidenceSought: Checked whether reducer writes both registry names; sampled reducer writes only registryPath/findings-registry.json and uses the long name as read fallback.
findingsNew.alternativeExplanation: The long filename may be a future canonical v(next) target, but current docs present it as live operator truth.
findingsNew.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade if command YAML copies or renames the short registry output to the long documented path after reducer execution.

findingsNew.title: External WebFetch content can reach a broad tool surface without prompt-injection or URL trust guardrails
Deep-research artifact writes are not contained to workspace/spec roots
Generated command contract assigns loop ownership to the LEAF agent
findingsNew.claim: The active compiled /deep:research command contract tells the executor that the deep-research LEAF owns the loop and every artifact write, while live packet sources say the YAML workflow owns the loop and the LEAF executes one iteration.
findingsNew.counterevidenceSought: Rendered contract still references YAML-owned assets and prompt-pack route proof, reducing severity from P0 but not removing the imperative contradiction.
findingsNew.alternativeExplanation: The generator may intend only to prevent inline research by the command executor; the wording should preserve YAML ownership instead of assigning whole-loop ownership to the LEAF.
findingsNew.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade if the compiled contract is proven never consumed by executors or the runtime ignores autonomousExecutionDirective and follows only YAML phases.

traceabilityChecks.package_skill_check: PASS; exact output captured in iteration narrative

findingsNew.title: No packet-level checklist for adding or changing a research-loop feature
findingsNew.scopeProof: Grep for '(adding? .*feature|new .*feature|maintainer|change map|touch .*files|update .*feature|feature.*checklist|when adding)' under the deep-research packet found changelog mentions and existing feature names only; no live maintainer checklist.
findingsNew.recommendation: Add a short maintainer checklist that maps feature changes across SKILL routing/resources, references, feature_catalog, manual_testing_playbook, command YAML/tests, assets, and scripts.

findingsNew.title: Reducer warns on valid findingsNew arrays
Graph convergence decisions are persisted without replayable signal payloads
Deep-review executor docs understate cli-opencode permission blast radius
findingsNew.claim: The deep-review packet tells operators that cli-opencode review iterations run with workspace-write sandboxing, but the live /deep:review command branches invoke opencode run with --dangerously-skip-permissions and explicitly state that sandboxMode='read-only' is not honored.
findingsNew.counterevidenceSought: Checked executor-audit env allowlisting and recursion guards plus prompt-pack allowed-write-path contract and write-boundary playbook; these reduce env/recursion and body-level risks but do not make the cli-opencode dangerous-permission branch match the documented workspace-write contract.
findingsNew.alternativeExplanation: The command may intentionally use --dangerously-skip-permissions because current OpenCode automation lacks an equivalent granular sandbox; the packet may rely on prompt-level write lists, post-dispatch validation, and tests rather than runtime permissions.
findingsNew.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade if the live command branch stops using --dangerously-skip-permissions or the packet documents dangerous permissions, marks reviewed target content as untrusted data, and adds an enforceable permission/path gate.

findingDetails.title: Deep-review executor docs understate cli-opencode permission blast radius
findingDetails.scopeProof: Exact searches for dangerously-skip-permissions, acceptEdits, untrusted, prompt injection, target content, ALLOWED WRITE PATHS, and SCOPE VIOLATION under the deep-review packet found the allowed-write-path contract and write-boundary tests, but no explicit untrusted-target-content mitigation in the prompt pack or executor-selection references.

findingsNew.title: Manual testing source anchors point to non-existent current lines
findingsNew.scopeProof: Scoped grep for SKILL.md quality-gate and deep/review.md layout references found stale command-flow scenario anchors under the deep-review packet, while live reads prove the referenced command and SKILL line ranges no longer exist.

traceabilityChecks.skillWordBudget: PASS: SKILL.md links completion_criteria.md and file exists
traceabilityChecks.completionCriteriaNamedGates: PASS_WITH_NOTE: nine core criteria present plus conditional security-sensitive override
traceabilityChecks.commandCrossReference: PASS: command wrapper renders compiled contract with current packet paths
traceabilityChecks.agentCrossReference: PASS: LEAF contract references current packet-limited writes

findingsNew.title: State-gate reference still presents the old three-gate STOP model as standalone truth
findingsNew.scopeProof: Scoped grep for gate-model terms found the current 9-gate blocked_stop model across README, convergence, loop protocol, state JSONL, state format, feature catalog, and tests, with stale three-gate/guard_violation wording concentrated in loop_state_and_gates.md and the quick-reference guard summary.

findingsNew.title: Literal fixture resolver breaks shipped sweep profiles on the model-benchmark loop-host path
findingsNew.claim: The public non-reviewer /deep:model-benchmark workflow routes profiles through loop-host's materialize-then-run pipeline, but shipped sweep profiles reference fixtures by parsed fixture id rather than by actual JSON filename; the wired materializer literal-resolves refs as <ref>.json and fails before scoring.
findingsNew.counterevidenceSought: Checked reviewer scorer routing and found command YAML intentionally bypasses loop-host for reviewer; checked sweep-benchmark and found it resolves fixtures by parsed id, but the active non-reviewer command path does not call sweep-benchmark.
findingsNew.alternativeExplanation: The sweep profiles may be intended for standalone sweep-benchmark only, but the public model-benchmark route accepts non-reviewer profile paths and sends them through the literal materializer.
findingsNew.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade to P2 if the command route is documented or changed to reject sweep profiles before loop-host, or if all shipped sweep profiles are moved behind a dedicated sweep-benchmark entry path that never uses the literal materializer.

traceabilityChecks.assetRename: pass: sampled four public/private fixture pairs and full parsed 98 JSON fixture files with zero scenarioId mismatches/incomplete pairs
traceabilityChecks.modelProfileRefs: partial: default literal refs pass; shipped sweep profile refs resolve by id for sweep-benchmark but fail literal materializer path
traceabilityChecks.runtimeTruthContracts: pass: sampled enum docs align with helper-owned lifecycle taxonomy
traceabilityChecks.paretoTradeoff: pass: active workflows use detectTradeOffs; unused checkParetoDominance not counted
traceabilityChecks.crossFileConsistency: partial: sampled script refs resolve; full 458-file packet not exhausted

findingsNew.title: Promotion and rollback boundaries trust supplied manifest/config equality instead of enforcing an allowed root
findingsNew.claim: The mutating promotion and rollback helpers enforce score/approval/hash gates, but their final write boundary is equality against caller-supplied config and manifest targets rather than realpath containment under an allowed root.
findingsNew.counterevidenceSought: Promotion requires proposalOnly=false, promotionEnabled=true, explicit approve, benchmark pass, repeatability pass, candidate existence, rubric guard, target/config equality, single manifest canonical target equality, and ship hash guards; rollback shared verifies accepted-state hashes. None of these constrain the resolved write target to an allowed root.
findingsNew.alternativeExplanation: Config and manifest may be intended as trusted operator-authored inputs, which lowers exploitability but does not provide an enforced write boundary in the reusable mutating CLI.
findingsNew.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade if the command/YAML layer proves it always constructs these paths from immutable trusted templates and rejects resolved targets outside an explicit allowlist before invoking helpers, or if helpers add realpath containment checks.

findingsNew.title: Model-benchmark mode-switch catalog still describes only two valid loop-host modes
findingsNew.claim: The live model-benchmark feature catalog entry says VALID_MODES holds only agent-improvement and model-benchmark, but current loop-host.cjs exposes four valid modes and adjacent Lane C/D catalog entries document the newer modes.
findingsNew.counterevidenceSought: Checked Lane C and Lane D feature-catalog entries plus the four improvement-lane command markdown files; those surfaces describe the current Lane C/D routes, so the issue appears isolated to this older Lane B feature entry rather than a command-level routing contradiction.
findingsNew.alternativeExplanation: The Lane B entry may have intentionally described only the original model-benchmark addition when it was authored. It is still a live feature-catalog entry rather than historical/changelog prose, so current readers can reasonably treat the closed-set claim as current truth.
findingsNew.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade to no finding if the catalog is explicitly marked historical or if the line is reworded as a historical note about the initial two-mode introduction.

findingsNew.title: New post-trim references are prose-linked but absent from the router map
findingsNew.claim: The three new post-trim reference files are discoverable by reading SKILL.md prose, but they are not present in the router RESOURCE_MAP or on-demand load set, so the packet's stated recursive/intent-based resource loading will not surface them for matching Lane A, Lane B, or runtime-truth tasks.

findingsNew.title: Persistence always records council completion as converged
findingsNew.claim: The full-report persistence path writes council_complete with convergence:true for every parsed report, so max-round non-convergence and all-seat failure reports can be durably recorded as converged even though the packet docs require non-converged or failed treatment.
findingsNew.counterevidenceSought: Checked parseCouncilReport, output_schema requiredness, config template, and completion advisory behavior for a separate convergence source or fail-closed gate; none was found in the reviewed surfaces.
findingsNew.alternativeExplanation: The helper may have been intended only for successful converged reports, but that precondition is not enforced and packet docs describe non-converged/failure persistence through the same state format.
findingsNew.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade to P2 if a caller-owned preflight is found that blocks persist-artifacts.cjs for non-converged or failed reports, or if another artifact is proven authoritative for all convergence consumers.

findingsNew.title: Completion docs and tests still describe a terminal council_complete event after audit events were added
README version metadata lags the current packet release
